The unconscious mind is infinitely more powerful than the conscious mind. There's no comparison. It's like, it's, you know what, I'll give you a great metaphor for this. Your conscious mind, what you're looking at right now, what you hear, what you're focusing on, my voice, the picture on the screen, right? Your inner dialogue that's talking to you as I speak, those that's, that's your conscious mind. That's like your RAM, your random access memory. That's like that little bit, that's the stuff that you can sort of access to do tasks in the moment, like on a computer, your RAM, your RAM, your random access memory, right? But you also have what? You also have a hard drive where all the old stuff is stored and that's where the power is in this massive hard drive, this database. Imagine right now, like almost like the internet itself is like the cloud, it's like that's the database where everything is stored. All the information is stored there and random access memory, conscious mind allows you to choose what you want to call on in your unconscious mind. So the power differential was not even close. That's why when we become unconsciously competent, it's like this, there's just no comparison. Like when you see Roger Federer or Tiger Woods in action and they're in the zone, it like almost seems like a pattern actually, like it's, how could that be? How does someone hit the ball so well? How do they do, how does anyone do something that well? Has the figure skater or downhill racer? They're not using their conscious mind. They're using their cerebellum, they're using the deepest reasons of their memory. These are parts of the brain that have incredibly robust, what you call data storage capabilities versus your conscious mind is nothing compared to that, nothing. So when you essentially tap into them, you become unconsciously competent. And when I talk about being a natural, born closer, what I'm saying is, is that when I would go into a sale, I was able to tap into the whole universe of everything I knew without even trying versus 99.99% of other salespeople, they have to consciously using that very limited power they have to figure it out. It's like, you almost can't walk into anything. How do you listen to the person? So what happens with the straight line is when you learn it, it's what it does is after a bit of practice, it actually, it makes the jump into your unconscious mind. That's how I turned all these knuckleheads into millionaires, into world-class puzzles. And I'm not saying you're a knucklehead, but that's how I will do it for you. It takes work. There's work to be done. You've got to practice, repeat. But what happens is this strategy allows you to cross it over into your unconscious mind. And once that happens, the potentials and then you have, you don't get it. I mean, maybe you don't understand the power that you're capable of. You don't understand it. I mean, because see, if you're not a natural, born salesman and you say, how can I possibly be like Jordan? You can. Because once you tap into your unconscious mind, hello, you could do me 10,000 of me.
